marketName: Museums, Historical Sites, and Similar Institutions

alternatives.name: Basic mechanical door alarm (no monitoring) — 45% share
alternatives.tradeoffs: No remote visibility; requires physical patrol to detect unreported activation; no audit log; no battery required for alarm

alternatives.name: Wired intruder detection + door contacts (alarm panel) — 25% share
alternatives.tradeoffs: Wiring through heritage fabric requires planning consent and conservation officer approval — major barrier in listed buildings; high retrofit cost

alternatives.name: Alarmed exit hardware with IoT monitoring (Türwächter) — 20% share
alternatives.tradeoffs: Proprietary wireless protocol (C6); battery dependency (C4); ~730 EUR/door (C7); surface-mount profile is accepted in heritage context

alternatives.name: Gallery attendant / security guard patrol
alternatives.tradeoffs: High recurring labor cost (security = ~22% of museum operating budget); coverage gaps when attendant is elsewhere; no event log; scales linearly with door count

alternatives.name: No dedicated exit monitoring (do nothing) — 10% share
alternatives.tradeoffs: Zero installation burden; zero P1 burden; compliance risk distributed across insurance, fire marshal, and grant-funding conditions
